title: Combined Work Rate
description: A can complete a piece of work in 12 days, and B can complete the same work in 18 days. If they work together, how many days will they take to complete the work?
explanation:
A's 1-day work = 1/12
B's 1-day work = 1/18
Combined 1-day work = 1/12 + 1/18 = (3 + 2) / 36 = 5 / 36
Time taken to complete the work = 36 / 5 = 7.2 days.
Shortcut formula: (A * B) / (A + B) = (12 * 18) / (12 + 18) = 216 / 30 = 7.2 days.

title: Early Departure
description: A, B, and C can complete a task in 10, 12, and 15 days respectively. They begin working together, but A leaves 2 days before the task is completed. In how many days was the task completed?
explanation:
Let the total work be LCM(10, 12, 15) = 60 units.
Efficiency of A = 60 / 10 = 6 units/day
Efficiency of B = 60 / 12 = 5 units/day
Efficiency of C = 60 / 15 = 4 units/day
Let the total time taken be x days.
Since A left 2 days before completion, A worked for (x - 2) days.
B and C worked for all x days.
Total work = 6 * (x - 2) + 5 * x + 4 * x = 60
=> 6x - 12 + 9x = 60
=> 15x = 72
=> x = 72 / 15 = 4.8 days.
Hence, the task was completed in 4.8 days.

title: Efficiency Ratios
description: A is twice as efficient as B and is therefore able to finish a piece of work in 30 days less than B. Working together, in how many days can they complete the work?
explanation:
Let the time taken by B to finish the work be x days.
Since A is twice as efficient as B, A takes half the time B takes, which is x/2 days.
Given that A takes 30 days less than B:
x - x/2 = 30
=> x/2 = 30
=> x = 60 days.
So B takes 60 days, and A takes 30 days.
A's 1-day work = 1/30
B's 1-day work = 1/60
Combined 1-day work = 1/30 + 1/60 = (2 + 1)/60 = 3/60 = 1/20.
Hence, working together they can complete the work in 20 days.

title: Alternate Day Schedule
description: A and B can complete a work in 9 and 12 days respectively. If they work on alternate days starting with A, in how many days will the work be finished?
explanation:
Total work = LCM(9, 12) = 36 units.
Efficiency of A = 36 / 9 = 4 units/day.
Efficiency of B = 36 / 12 = 3 units/day.
In a 2-day cycle (Day 1: A, Day 2: B), they complete = 4 + 3 = 7 units.
In 10 days (5 full cycles), work completed = 5 * 7 = 35 units.
Remaining work = 36 - 35 = 1 unit.
On Day 11, it is A's turn. A's efficiency is 4 units/day.
Time taken by A to complete 1 unit = 1/4 = 0.25 days.
Total time taken = 10 + 0.25 = 10.25 days.

title: Composite Work Groups
description: 4 men and 6 women can complete a work in 8 days, while 3 men and 7 women can complete it in 10 days. In how many days can 10 women complete the work alone?
explanation:
Let 1 man's 1-day work be M and 1 woman's 1-day work be W.
From the given data:
8 * (4M + 6W) = 1 (Total work)
=> 32M + 48W = 1
Also:
10 * (3M + 7W) = 1
=> 30M + 70W = 1
Equating total work:
32M + 48W = 30M + 70W
=> 2M = 22W
=> 1M = 11W (A man's efficiency is equal to 11 women's efficiency).
Substitute M in first equation:
32(11W) + 48W = 1
=> 352W + 48W = 1
=> 400W = 1
=> 1W = 1/400 (A woman's 1-day work is 1/400).
10 women's 1-day work = 10 * (1/400) = 1/40.
Therefore, 10 women will take 40 days to complete the work.
